# VLTG

> A free, full-length IBEW electrical-apprenticeship aptitude practice test with an instant 1-9 stanine diagnostic and a personalized study plan.

VLTG helps people preparing for the IBEW / electrical Training ALLIANCE (formerly NJATC) apprenticeship aptitude test. Most prep sites paywall their practice tests. VLTG gives you the complete test for free, scores it, and builds a study plan around your weakest skills.

## About the real test (accurate reference)
- Two sections: Algebra & Functions (33 questions) and Reading Comprehension (36 questions), 69 questions total.
- Time on the real exam: about 46 minutes for math and 51 minutes for reading.
- Scoring is a stanine: a normalized 1-9 scale based on percentile rank, where 5 is average and 4 is the minimum to qualify for an interview.
- No prior electrical knowledge is required; the math is generic high-school algebra and the reading passages cover everyday topics.

## What VLTG offers
- The full 69-question practice test, free, no credit card, self-paced.
- An instant diagnostic on the 1-9 stanine scale, plus an estimated percentile.
- A pace benchmark comparing your time to the real section limits.
- A prioritized, skill-by-skill study plan built from your answers.

## Pages
- Home: https://vltg.net (take the free practice test)
- FAQ: https://vltg.net/faq (what the test is, how many questions, what score you need, what a stanine is, retake waits, and how this practice test is built)
- About: https://vltg.net/about (who built it and why)
- More guides are in progress.

## Common questions, answered
- **How many questions and how long?** 69 questions in about 97 minutes: 33 math in 46 minutes and 36 reading in 51 minutes. Roughly 85 seconds per question, no calculator.
- **What score do you need?** A 4 or better on the 1-9 stanine scale qualifies you for an interview at most locals. 5 is average. Locals rank applicants by score, so higher usually means an earlier call.
- **Do you need electrical knowledge?** No. The math is ordinary high-school algebra and the passages cover everyday topics. It is an aptitude test, not a knowledge test.
- **What is a stanine?** A 1-9 score based on where you land relative to other test takers, not the percentage you got right. 5 is average by definition; 70% correct does not mean a 7.
- **How long until you can retake it?** It depends on the local. Three months is the standard minimum, plenty require six, and some require a full year.
- **Is it affiliated with the IBEW?** No. VLTG is independent and not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to the IBEW, NECA, or the electrical Training ALLIANCE.

## Who built it
VLTG is built by Bhavjot Khurana, who works in learning measurement rather than the electrical trade: a math and statistics background, 700+ tutoring sessions, and a former data analyst at an education company. The premise is that people are bad at self-diagnosis. Miss an algebra question that has fractions in it and pass one that doesn't, and the problem is fractions, not algebra. The test exists to identify which skill is actually the gap.

## Methodology and limitations (stated plainly)
- All questions are original, written for VLTG. The real exam's items are secured and not public, so no practice product anywhere has the real questions.
- Math answers are verified programmatically before being served, correct answers are distributed evenly across A/B/C/D, and reading passages are original.
- The stanine score is an ESTIMATE. The official conversion tables are proprietary to the American Institutes for Research and are not public, so VLTG implements the standard stanine method against a documented estimate of applicant performance and displays the percentile alongside it. It will be recalibrated as real response data accumulates.
- VLTG is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to the IBEW, NECA, or the electrical Training ALLIANCE.
